plan:
Step 1: Add PresetName type and preset snapshot definitions to src/domain/presets.ts
- Create src/domain/presets.ts with:
  - `PresetName` union type: 'fast' | 'mixed' | 'poor' (no unused Preset interface)
  - `PRESET_SNAPSHOTS: Record<PresetName, NetworkSnapshot>` with these exact values:
    - FAST: { medianRtt: 20, jitter: 5, packetLoss: 0, bandwidth: 50, samples: [], timestampMs: 0 }
    - MIXED: { medianRtt: 350, jitter: 150, packetLoss: 0.10, bandwidth: 10, samples: [], timestampMs: 0 }
    - POOR: { medianRtt: 950, jitter: 450, packetLoss: 0.50, bandwidth: 1, samples: [], timestampMs: 0 }
  - These values produce clearly differentiated SceneParams through mapSnapshotToScene:
    - FAST → height=0.98, fracture=0.007, ruinLevel=0.009
    - MIXED → height=0.65, fracture=0.24, ruinLevel=0.235
    - POOR → height=0.05, fracture=0.78, ruinLevel=0.755
- Export PresetName and PRESET_SNAPSHOTS from src/domain/index.ts
- Files: src/domain/presets.ts (new), src/domain/index.ts (edit)

Step 2: Create preset state manager at src/domain/presetState.ts
- Create a simple state holder with:
  - `createPresetState(initial?: PresetName)` factory returning a `PresetState` object
  - `PresetState.select(name: PresetName): NetworkSnapshot` — sets the active preset, returns the corresponding NetworkSnapshot
  - `PresetState.current(): { name: PresetName; snapshot: NetworkSnapshot } | null` — returns current selection or null if no preset active
  - `PresetState.subscribe(cb: (snapshot: NetworkSnapshot, name: PresetName) => void): () => void` — registers a listener called on select(), returns unsubscribe function
- This is the integration point: downstream consumers (render loop, future sampler) subscribe to snapshot changes. When a preset is selected, subscribers receive the new NetworkSnapshot immediately.
- Keep it pure TypeScript with no DOM dependency so it stays in src/domain/.
- Export from src/domain/index.ts
- Files: src/domain/presetState.ts (new), src/domain/index.ts (edit)

Step 3: Create preset selector UI component at src/ui/presetSelector.ts
- Create `initPresetSelector(overlay: HTMLDivElement, state: PresetState): void`
- Renders three buttons into the overlay: 'Fast', 'Mixed', 'Poor' with data-preset attributes ('fast', 'mixed', 'poor')
- Buttons container has `pointer-events: auto` (since overlay is pointer-events: none)
- Each button click calls `state.select(presetName)`
- Highlights the active button with an 'active' CSS class
- Minimal inline styles or a small style block for positioning (top-right corner of overlay)
- Files: src/ui/presetSelector.ts (new)

Step 4: Wire preset state into main.ts
- Import createPresetState and initPresetSelector
- Create preset state instance: `const presetState = createPresetState()`
- Call `initPresetSelector(overlay, presetState)` to mount the UI
- Subscribe to preset state changes: `presetState.subscribe((snapshot) => { ... })` — for now, log to console or store as current snapshot for future render loop integration
- This completes the acceptance criterion 'Preset switch updates current snapshot' — selecting a preset updates the shared state and notifies subscribers
- Files: src/main.ts (edit)

Step 5: Unit tests for preset definitions — tests/unit/domain/presets.test.ts
- Test that PRESET_SNAPSHOTS contains exactly keys 'fast', 'mixed', 'poor'
- Test each preset produces expected SceneParams ranges through mapSnapshotToScene:
  - FAST: height > 0.9, fracture < 0.05, ruinLevel < 0.05
  - POOR: height < 0.1, fracture > 0.7, ruinLevel > 0.7
  - MIXED: height between 0.4 and 0.8 (clearly between fast and poor), fracture between 0.1 and 0.5
- Test that all three presets produce all SceneParams in [0, 1]
- Test monotonicity: fast.height > mixed.height > poor.height, poor.fracture > mixed.fracture > fast.fracture
- Files: tests/unit/domain/presets.test.ts (new)

Step 6: Unit tests for preset state manager — tests/unit/domain/presetState.test.ts
- Test select() returns the correct NetworkSnapshot for each preset name
- Test current() returns null before any selection, returns correct state after select()
- Test subscribe() callback fires on select() with correct snapshot and name
- Test unsubscribe stops notifications
- Test switching presets updates current() and notifies subscribers
- Files: tests/unit/domain/presetState.test.ts (new)

Step 7: E2E smoke test for preset selector — tests/e2e/presets.spec.ts
- Test that three preset buttons are visible on page load (locator: [data-preset='fast'], [data-preset='mixed'], [data-preset='poor'])
- Test clicking each button adds 'active' class to the clicked button
- Test clicking a different button moves 'active' class
- Files: tests/e2e/presets.spec.ts (new)

Step 8: Run full quality gate and fix any issues
- Run: npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test && npm run test:e2e
- Fix any lint, type, or test failures
- Ensure all existing tests still pass (no regressions)
testing_recommendations: Unit test: Verify FAST preset (RTT=20, jitter=5, loss=0) produces height>0.9, fracture<0.05 through mapSnapshotToScene
Unit test: Verify POOR preset (RTT=950, jitter=450, loss=0.50) produces height<0.1, fracture>0.7, ruinLevel>0.7 through mapSnapshotToScene
Unit test: Verify MIXED preset (RTT=350, jitter=150, loss=0.10) produces height between 0.4–0.8 and fracture between 0.1–0.5, clearly intermediate
Unit test: Verify monotonicity — fast.height > mixed.height > poor.height and poor.fracture > mixed.fracture > fast.fracture
Unit test: All preset SceneParams values are in [0, 1] range
Unit test: presetState.select() returns correct snapshot, current() tracks state, subscribe/unsubscribe work correctly
E2E test: Three preset buttons with data-preset attributes are visible on page load
E2E test: Clicking a preset button activates it (adds 'active' class) and deactivates others
Run full quality gate: npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test && npm run test:e2e
risks: The overlay div has pointer-events: none — preset buttons must be in a child container with pointer-events: auto to be clickable

Preset state is not yet consumed by the render loop (no SceneParams-driven rendering exists yet) — this ticket wires up the state and subscription mechanism but visual feedback depends on future tickets (US-010+)
timestampMs: 0 and empty samples[] in preset snapshots may cause issues if downstream code iterates samples — presets should document that samples are intentionally empty for deterministic use
E2E tests depend on Vite dev server being available — ensure playwright.config.ts has webServer configured correctly
